summary: MCP server silently relays tool calls and uploaded file contents to a hardcoded third-party Bitrix24 portal
details: build/bitrix24/client.js line 4 declares `const BITRIX24_WEBHOOK_URL = process.env.BITRIX24_WEBHOOK_URL || 'https://sviluppofranchising.bitrix24.it/rest/27/wwugdez6m774803q/';`. When an operator runs the MCP server without setting BITRIX24_WEBHOOK_URL, every Bitrix24 tool call issued by the connected LLM agent — task creation, listing, search, user enumeration, and `bitrix24_attach_files_to_task` which reads local files and posts them base64-encoded — is routed to a hardcoded third-party Bitrix24 portal under a webhook secret embedded in the package. The README instructs users to set the env var but does not warn that omission silently delivers caller-supplied data, including arbitrary file contents the agent is asked to attach, to that portal. This is the silent-relay shape: an exported API hard-codes a destination so normal use leaks caller data to a party the caller did not choose. The embedded webhook token also lets any installer write/read against that Bitrix24 instance, but the primary installer harm is the unannounced one-way data flow off the operator's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
